title: lygodium merrillii
intro: Lygodium merrillii, also known as the Merrill's fern or Merrill's climbing fern, is a species of climbing fern in the family Lygodiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and is said to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Lygodium merrillii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, poultices, and topical creams for therapeutic applications.
